trials.justification: All 7 rubric criteria met: (1) 7 business rules identified covering KYC gating, daily limits, settlement finality, sanctions screening, account lockout, notification, and unverified receive-only. (2) Notification rule (BR-6) correctly marked invariant: false with clear justification. (3) Sanctions rule (BR-4) marked invariant: true. (4) Timezone/daily-limit-window ambiguity explicitly surfaced as an escalation with options. (5) 12 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria (well above the 4 minimum). (6) Confidence level 72, within the 60-85 range, with detailed assumptions listed. Additionally, Bird correctly distinguished regulatory invariants from operational rules — BR-2 (specific dollar amounts) marked invariant: false while the existence of limits is enforced, which shows nuanced understanding.

observations.text: Excellent invariant classification: BR-1, BR-3, BR-4, BR-5 correctly invariant: true; BR-2 (dollar amounts), BR-6 (notification), BR-7 (receive policy) correctly invariant: false with clear justifications
Timezone ambiguity for daily limit surfaced as a dedicated escalation with three concrete options and a recommendation (UTC midnight)
12 acceptance criteria with well-constructed Given/When/Then scenarios covering all major rules including edge boundaries (exactly $1,000, exactly $5,000)
Rich edge cases section covering mid-day KYC upgrade, sanctions list changes during pending settlement, login counter reset behavior, and self-transfers
Sanctions screening timing escalation goes beyond the rubric requirement — identifies the initiation-vs-settlement gap which is a real compliance concern
Business impact analysis is thorough with specific penalty ranges (OFAC $50K-$20M+) and stakeholder mapping
BR-1 combines KYC-required-for-sending and unverified-receive-only into one rule, while BR-7 separates out the receive permission — slight structural redundancy but not a misclassification
Confidence level of 72 is well-calibrated given the number of spec gaps identified, with clear separation of high and low confidence areas

trials.justification: All 7 required rules identified (8 total, with daily limits split by tier). Notification rule (BR-8) correctly marked invariant: false. Sanctions screening (BR-6) correctly marked invariant: true. Timezone ambiguity for daily limits explicitly surfaced in both edge_cases and escalations. 15 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria far exceed the 4 minimum. Confidence level of 72 is within the 60-85 band with detailed assumptions listed. Daily limit rules (BR-3, BR-4) marked invariant: false — the rubric says existence of limits is invariant but specific amounts are soft config; Bird's classification focuses on the dollar amounts being adjustable, which is a defensible interpretation. No failure modes triggered: sanctions rule present, notification rule not marked invariant, timezone gap surfaced, no indiscriminate invariant marking.

observations.text: All 7 required business rules identified with an 8th (partially-verified limits) providing additional granularity
Notification rule (BR-8) correctly classified as invariant: false with clear justification distinguishing it from regulatory requirements
Sanctions screening (BR-6) correctly classified as invariant: true with strong justification citing criminal liability and license revocation
Timezone ambiguity explicitly called out in edge_cases and elevated to a formal escalation with three options and a recommendation (UTC)
15 acceptance criteria — well above the 4 minimum — covering boundary conditions, reset logic, and lock persistence
Confidence level of 72 is well-calibrated, with clear separation of high-confidence vs low-confidence areas
Identified additional ambiguities beyond the rubric: partially-verified withdrawal rights, inbound sanctions screening, pre-settlement reversal mechanics, notification delivery guarantees
Edge case for race condition on concurrent transfers (atomic daily limit enforcement) shows strong domain understanding
AC-15 tests consecutive failure counter reset on successful login — a subtle correctness scenario not in the rubric

Daily limit rules (BR-3, BR-4) marked invariant: false — rubric states existence of tiered limits is invariant: true (only specific dollar amounts are soft config). Bird's justification is reasonable but doesn't distinguish between the structural requirement (limits must exist per tier) and the configurable threshold
